Based on the retrieved evidence, there is overwhelming support that strain specifically denotes the deformation experienced by materials or biological tissues under the application of external forces. This supports option B stating "a deformation resulting from an applied load." While options A, C, and D touch tangentially on elements of strain definitions, only B precisely captures the essence of what happens when external forces cause changes in form—regardless of whether those changes represent tensile strength (option A), oppositional resistance (options C&D). Therefore, my answer remains consistent with the earlier reasoning favoring option B.
